mechanism_narrative: A lower level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance against a zero-mean null hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0%, it signals severe labor market slack and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces corporate earnings revisions downward and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the conditional mean return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during periods of capacity constraints, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO growth drops below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% forces the Federal Reserve to consider rate hikes, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on inflation prints above 0.4% due to growth optimism, or the negative return signal fails to materialize in 70% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and rising household income, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns when accompanied by wage stagnation, or the 60-day forward return correlation with PAYEMS changes falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling retail sales indicates inventory overhang and weakening downstream demand, which triggers earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive returns when supply chains normalize, or the interaction effect loses predictive significance in a walk-forward validation framework.
